optionA.text: Abdominal uncini form nearly complete cinctures around the body

optionB.text: Abdominal uncini in short, discrete tori

optionA.text: Thoracic uncini with long, gently curved shafts (acicular); companion setae always absent

optionB.text: Thoracic uncini with short or long, but always strongly bent shafts (avicular); companion setae present in some forms

optionA.text: Abdomen with two or three setigers

optionB.text: Abdomen with four or more setigers

optionA.text: Radioles partially united by a membrane

optionB.text: Radioles free to the base

optionA.text: With an extensive lower lip, curved up under the weakly bilobed prostomium

optionB.text: Without a distinct lower lip; prostomium anteriorly rounded

optionA.text: Abdominal uncini short-handled

optionB.text: Abdominal uncini long-handled

optionA.text: Without ventral vascularized filaments

optionB.text: With ventral vascularized filaments in the tentacular crown

optionA.text: Ventral vascularized filaments branched; three pairs of radioli

optionB.text: Ventral vascularized filaments smooth; two pairs of radioli

optionA.text: Posterior end distinctly modified, with fused segments or greatly expanded pygidium

optionB.text: Posterior end not modified

optionA.text: Seven last setigers covered dorsally by the large, ring-shaped pygidium

optionB.text: Last several setigers flanged laterally to form a spoon-shaped dorsal cavity

optionA.text: Radioles united by a membrane

optionB.text: Radioles free to the base

optionA.text: Abdominal uncini with a large main fang and a crest of smaller teeth

optionB.text: Abdominal uncini with major teeth in two or more rows

optionA.text: More than ten pairs of radioli in the tentacular crown

optionB.text: Maximally five pairs of radioli in the tentacular crown

optionA.text: Radioles externally distinctly ridged

optionB.text: Radioles externally rounded

optionA.text: Thoracic neuropodial companion setae absent

optionB.text: Thoracic neuropodial companion setae present

optionA.text: Radioli with external stylodes

optionB.text: Radioles without external stylodes

optionA.text: Four thoracic setigers; external stylodes very small

optionB.text: Eight thoracic setigers; external stylodes well developed

optionA.text: Thoracic spatulate notosetae present

optionB.text: Thoracic spatulate notosetae absent

optionA.text: Thoracic uncini with gently curved, long handles

optionB.text: Thoracic uncini with sharply bent handles

optionA.text: Collar reduced, radioli basally united by a web

optionB.text: Collar well developed; radioli free to the base

optionA.text: Tentacular crown with radioli arranged in one or two spirals

optionB.text: Tentacular crown with radioli in two semicircles

optionA.text: Posterior thoracic notopodia with lancet-shaped, transversely striated setae

optionB.text: Lancet-shaped notopodial setae absent

optionA.text: Collar two-lobed

optionB.text: Collar four-lobed

optionA.text: Anterior ventrum irregularly rugose

optionB.text: Anterior ventrum smooth, apart from the glandular fields

optionA.text: One half of tentacular crown very much larger than the other; spatulate setae absent

optionB.text: Both parts of the tentacular crown similar in size; spatulate setae present

optionA.text: Large subdistal compound eyes on a few dorsal radioli

optionB.text: Eyes, if present, smaller and scattered over most of the radioli

optionA.text: Collar reduced or absent, a triangular ventral projection at base of tentacular crown present

optionB.text: Collar well developed; triangular ventral projection absent

optionA.text: Notosetae of the collar-segment in a long row

optionB.text: Notosetae of the collar-segment in a tuft

optionA.text: Radioli dichotomously branching

optionB.text: Radioli not divided

optionA.text: Spatulate thoracic notosetae absent

optionB.text: Spatulate thoracic notosetae present

optionA.text: Thoracic uncini at least in part nearly straight and long-handled

optionB.text: Thoracic uncini sharply bent, long- or short-handled

optionA.text: Abdominal uncini long-handled and nearly straight

optionB.text: Abdominal uncini short-handled and strongly bent

optionA.text: Thoracic uncini long-handled

optionB.text: Thoracic uncini short-handled
